mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and consumer price inflation indicate robust demand pulling through to corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise together, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening economic output that boosts near-term equity valuations, but this mechanism only activates during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during stable policy periods, or the predictive relationship reverses direction when policy volatility exceeds historical median.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation beyond the 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >70% of cases following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows no predictive power for 5-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes negative when tested against historical market data.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals broad economic expansion that lifts corporate profits, but this transmission mechanism only operates effectively during early-to-mid cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-to-mid cycle phases, or the relationship inverts during late-cycle periods as specified.
